description: The new moon is a time of darkness and stillness, marking the beginning of a new lunar cycle. It carries the energy of potential, a blank page waiting for your intentions. This is the most powerful phase for planting seeds of change and turning inward to listen.
rituals: Set intentions for the lunar cycle
Write a list of goals or desires on fresh paper
Perform a candle-lighting ceremony for new beginnings
Create a vision board or intention jar
Meditate in complete darkness to connect with inner vision
Cleanse your altar and sacred space for the cycle ahead
intentions: I welcome new opportunities with an open heart
I plant seeds of transformation that will grow in their own time
I trust the darkness as a place of fertile potential
I release the previous cycle and begin again with clarity
activities: Journaling about what you wish to call in
Taking a ritual bath with intention-setting herbs
Starting a new creative project or practice
Pulling tarot cards for the month ahead
Quiet reflection and solitude
Planting seeds, literal or metaphorical
avoid: Launching or publicising major projects; let them germinate first
Overcommitting your energy or social calendar
Rushing decisions that need time to take root

description: The waxing crescent is the first sliver of light returning after the new moon, carrying the energy of hope and forward momentum. Your intentions have been set, and now is the time to nurture them with action and belief. This phase asks you to commit to what you started and take the first tangible steps.
rituals: Affirm your new moon intentions aloud each morning
Light a green or white candle to encourage growth
Craft a charm bag with herbs and crystals aligned to your goals
Perform a dedication ritual to your chosen path
Water and tend to any plants as a sympathetic act of nurturing
intentions: I take steady steps toward what I have called in
I trust the process even when results are not yet visible
I nourish my dreams with patience and commitment
My courage grows alongside the light of the moon
activities: Making concrete plans and timelines for your goals
Gathering resources and supplies you will need
Reaching out to allies or mentors who support your vision
Gentle movement practices like yoga or walking in nature
Reading or studying subjects related to your intentions
Tending a garden or houseplants
avoid: Self-doubt or abandoning intentions too early
Comparing your beginning to someone else's middle
Drastic changes in direction; stay the course

description: The first quarter moon is half-lit and carries a decisive, action-oriented energy. Challenges and obstacles may surface now; they are not setbacks but invitations to strengthen your resolve. This is the phase of commitment, where you push through resistance and make choices that align with your intentions.
rituals: Perform a courage or strength spell
Write down obstacles and burn the paper to release resistance
Charge a talisman or piece of jewellery with determination
Do a crossroads ritual to affirm the direction you have chosen
Light an orange or red candle for willpower and action
intentions: I face challenges as opportunities to grow stronger
I make decisions with confidence and clarity
I am resilient and adaptable in pursuit of what matters
I choose action over hesitation
Obstacles refine my path; they do not block it
activities: Tackling difficult tasks or conversations you have been avoiding
Making firm decisions and committing to a direction
Physical exercise or vigorous movement to channel restless energy
Reorganising your workspace or schedule for efficiency
Breaking larger goals into actionable steps
Asserting boundaries where needed
avoid: Giving up at the first sign of difficulty
Indecision or trying to keep every option open
Ignoring the friction; it is showing you something important

description: The waxing gibbous moon is nearly full, shining with an energy of refinement and fine-tuning. The hard work has been done, and now is the time to adjust, polish, and trust the process. This phase invites patience and gratitude as your intentions approach manifestation.
rituals: Review and refine your intentions from the new moon
Perform a gratitude ritual for progress already made
Anoint candles with oils that correspond to your goals
Practice divination to check alignment with your path
Offer thanks to guides, ancestors, or the earth for their support
Create or refresh a gratitude altar
intentions: I trust that what I have begun is unfolding as it should
I refine my efforts with care and attention
I am grateful for the progress I have already made
I remain open to adjustments that serve my highest good
activities: Editing, revising, or improving existing work
Practising patience and trusting timing
Spending time in nature to ground and centre
Expressing gratitude through writing or art
Fine-tuning details of projects nearing completion
Sharing your progress with a trusted circle
avoid: Perfectionism that stalls forward movement
Starting entirely new projects; focus on what is already in motion
Anxiety about outcomes; the seeds are growing beneath the surface

description: The full moon is the peak of the lunar cycle, a time of illumination, heightened energy, and powerful manifestation. Everything is lit up, including what has been hidden. This is the most potent phase for spellwork, celebration, charging tools, and releasing what no longer serves you.
rituals: Charge crystals, tools, and moon water under the full moon
Perform a release or letting-go ceremony
Hold a full moon circle with others or in solitude
Write down what you wish to release and burn or bury the paper
Dance, sing, or celebrate the fullness of your power
Perform divination for deep insight and clarity
intentions: I release what no longer serves my growth
I celebrate how far I have come
I stand in the fullness of my power and truth
I let the light of the moon illuminate what needs to be seen
I am whole, radiant, and aligned
activities: Hosting or attending a moon circle or ritual gathering
Making moon water by leaving water under moonlight
Taking a moonlit walk or spending time outdoors at night
Performing your most important spellwork or manifestation rituals
Emotional release through art, movement, or journaling
Forgiveness work, of self or others
avoid: Suppressing strong emotions; let them move through you
Beginning brand-new ventures; this energy is for culmination
Making impulsive decisions fuelled by heightened emotion

description: The waning gibbous moon begins the descent from fullness, carrying an energy of gratitude, sharing, and integration. The peak has passed, and now is the time to absorb what you have learned and give back from your abundance. This is sometimes called the disseminating moon, a phase of teaching and reflection.
rituals: Write a gratitude list for the blessings of this cycle
Share knowledge or wisdom with your community
Make offerings to the earth, ancestors, or spirits
Perform a self-blessing or anointing ritual
Light a pink or white candle for compassion and generosity
intentions: I share my gifts and knowledge generously
I integrate the lessons this cycle has brought me
I am grateful for the abundance in my life
I give back to the world that sustains me
I honour what the full moon revealed
activities: Teaching, mentoring, or sharing what you know
Volunteering or acts of service
Reflecting on what the full moon illuminated
Decluttering and donating items you no longer need
Cooking nourishing meals and sharing them with others
Writing about insights and lessons learned
avoid: Hoarding energy, resources, or knowledge
Ignoring the insights that surfaced at the full moon
Resisting the natural slowing of energy

description: The last quarter moon is half-lit once more, mirroring the first quarter but turned inward. This is a phase of release, forgiveness, and clearing away what is no longer needed. Old patterns, stale habits, and unfinished emotional business are ready to be composted and returned to the earth.
rituals: Perform a banishing spell for habits or patterns you are releasing
Cleanse your home with smoke, sound, or salt water
Write a forgiveness letter, to yourself or another, and release it
Cut energetic cords with a cord-cutting ritual
Bury or burn symbolic objects that represent what you are letting go
Take a cleansing bath with salt and purifying herbs
intentions: I release old patterns that have outlived their purpose
I forgive freely and make space for renewal
I clear away what clutters my spirit and my life
I trust that releasing creates room for something better
activities: Deep cleaning and physically clearing your space
Ending or completing projects and tying up loose ends
Having honest conversations that have been postponed
Breaking a habit or routine that no longer serves you
Cord-cutting meditations and energy clearing practices
Composting, literally and metaphorically
avoid: Clinging to what is clearly ready to go
Starting new commitments or taking on new responsibilities
Filling the space immediately; sit with the emptiness

description: The waning crescent is the final whisper of light before the moon disappears entirely, carrying a deep energy of rest, surrender, and spiritual communion. This is the most introspective phase of the cycle, a time to withdraw, dream, and restore before the next new moon begins the cycle again.
rituals: Perform a dream incubation ritual before sleep
Sit in stillness and meditate on what the cycle has taught you
Anoint yourself with calming oils and rest intentionally
Practice automatic writing or stream-of-consciousness journaling
Light a black or dark blue candle for deep rest and psychic receptivity
Leave an offering of thanks for the closing cycle
intentions: I surrender to rest and trust the fallow time
I honour the wisdom that comes through stillness and dreams
I release the need to be productive and simply be
I prepare my spirit gently for the renewal ahead
I listen to the quiet voice within
activities: Sleeping deeply and paying attention to dreams
Spending time alone in quiet contemplation
Taking long baths or practising restorative self-care
Reviewing your journal entries from the full cycle
Gentle stretching or yin yoga
Listening to ambient music or sitting in silence
avoid: Pushing yourself to be active or productive
Making major decisions; wait for the new moon
Ignoring your body's need for rest and recuperation
